issues_found: AIS=0.566 in paper (Section 4.3, abstract) is fabricated. Actual measured value is AIS=0.123 (Spearman rho=0.195) from metrics_results_v2.json. LOO-CV R^2=-0.18 (zero out-of-sample predictive power). This was flagged as critical in iter_014 and not fixed in iter_015.

CWD K_d mapping in Table 1 claims 'K_d > 0, Derivative only.' Fitting data (200-epoch, 3-seed) shows K_p=0.0000, K_i=-0.0000, K_d=-0.0000, scale=0.5000. The 4.71% fit is entirely from WD magnitude halving, not derivative gain. Table 1 contains an empirically falsified claim. Flagged iter_014, not fixed iter_015.

CSI has four contradictory formulas across Section 4.2, Table 6 caption, normalization paragraph, and computation code. CSI_temporal=-5.75 for UDWDC is impossible under any stated formula (all produce non-negative values). The paper's central instability finding is unverifiable. Flagged since iter_007, 4+ iterations recurring.

Theorem 1 and Propositions 2-3 stated without proofs for 7+ iterations. Paper claims 'Theoretical analysis' as Contribution 4. Theorem 1 claims 'strictly tighter generalization bounds' -- a strong quantitative claim requiring formal derivation. Lyapunov V_t empirically increases, contradicting Theorem 1's guarantee.

ImageNet experiments are 12/40 complete. SWD, DefazioCorrective, NoWD have zero 90-epoch results. CWD has 1 seed (no CI). Without NoWD, BEM is uncomputable at ImageNet scale. The paper's primary novel metric is undemonstrable at its largest scale. Flagged since iter_004, 11+ iterations unresolved.

CWD halved-lambda ablation never executed despite being flagged P0 for 4 iterations. FixedWD @ lambda=5e-5 vs CWD @ lambda=1e-4 on CIFAR-10 would definitively resolve whether CWD's behavior is alignment information or magnitude reduction. This is ~1 GPU-hour on an 8-GPU machine.

CPR's 3.02pp ImageNet advantage over FixedWD is confounded by 2.3x WD budget difference. Without budget-matched controls (FixedWD at lambda=2e-4/3e-4, 90 epochs), the paper cannot distinguish 'better WD allocation' from 'simply stronger regularization.' This is the most important unresolved question for the paper's core mechanistic claim.

UDWDC-v2 BN layer bug produces 205,000x WD budget (98,599 vs FixedWD's 0.48). Floor clipping applies lambda_min to all 65 layers including 41 BN/bias layers. BN layers are scale-invariant, so WD on them is meaningless. All v2 BEM values are contaminated. One-line code fix flagged for 3+ iterations.

Title 'Unified Feedback Control Framework' overpromises: 2/5 methods (SWD 45.81%, DefazioCorrective 37.56%) fail fitting. CWD fit explained by scale=0.5, not PID gains. Only CPR (9.57%) genuinely captured by PID parameters. The framework unifies 1-2 of 5 methods.

UDWDC listed as Contribution 2 ('simple proportional controller') but underperforms NoWD on CIFAR-10 (90.15% vs 90.25%) and FixedWD on all benchmarks. Listing a method that harms performance as a contribution is overclaiming.
issues_fixed: AIS claim of 0.566 in paper.md Section 4.3 and abstract has no backing data. Cross-validation found: metrics_results_v2.json reports AIS=0.123 (Spearman rho=0.195); alignment_informativeness pilot shows all LOO-CV R-squared values are negative (alpha_bar R^2=-0.235). The value 0.566 appears in no result file. Paper also claims r_alpha_gengap=0.698 contradicting R^2=-0.235 from pilot. This is a fabricated headline metric.

CWD gain-mapping in Table 1 claims 'CWD corresponds to K_d > 0, Derivative only.' Fitting data (unifying_table.md, 200-epoch 3-seed) shows K_p=0.0000, K_i=-0.0000, K_d=-0.0000, offset=-5.014e-5, scale=0.5000. The 4.71% fitting error is achieved entirely via scale=0.5 (WD magnitude halving), not K_d. The unification claim for CWD is directly contradicted by the paper's own experiment output.

ImageNet results cover only 4 of 7 methods (12/40 runs completed, stopped by user). SWD, DefazioCorrective, NoWD have zero 90-epoch results. CWD has 1 seed (no CI). Without NoWD, BEM is uncomputable at ImageNet scale. Paper presents Table 5 as 'comprehensive experiments' while 3 methods and the primary novel metric are missing.

CWD vs halved-lambda ablation was identified as 'essential future work' in Section 6.3 and planned in the proposal (Phase 2) but never executed. Without it, CWD's behavior cannot be attributed to alignment information vs WD magnitude reduction. This is a 2 GPU-hour experiment on an 8-GPU machine that ran experiments for days. The 'compute constraints' excuse is not credible.

Theorem 1, Proposition 2, and Proposition 3 are stated without formal proofs or proof sketches. Paper claims 'Theoretical analysis' as Contribution 4. For a NeurIPS submission, missing proofs for stated theorems will trigger desk rejection from theory-oriented reviewers. Theorem 1 claims 'strictly tighter generalization bounds' -- a strong quantitative claim requiring formal derivation.

CSI formula has three contradictory definitions across the paper: (1) Section 4.2: CSI_temporal = 1/Var_t[lambda_eff]; (2) Table 6 caption: CSI = 1/(1 + Var_t[lambda/mean]); (3) metrics_results.json reports UDWDC CSI_temporal = -5.75 -- impossible under either stated formula (both produce only positive values). The actual computation code for 200-epoch refined CSI is unidentifiable. Any reviewer checking the formulas will flag this.

PID unification claim is overstated: fitting fails for 2/5 methods (SWD 45.81% error, DefazioCorrective 37.56%). CWD's extended fit has near-zero PID gains (all ~1e-7), driven by scale=0.5 parameter. Title 'Unified Feedback Control Framework' is inaccurate when 40% of methods fall outside the framework. The taxonomy is solid but the 'unified' label is misleading.

UDWDC underperforms FixedWD on ALL benchmarks (CIFAR-10: -0.53pp, CIFAR-100: -2.01pp, ImageNet: -1.79pp) and even underperforms NoWD on CIFAR-10. UDWDC-v2 has a pathological 205,000x WD budget from applying floor clipping to BN layers. The paper's algorithmic contribution has negative practical value but is still listed as 'Contribution 2.'

Figure 4 (alignment_snr.pdf) uses 10-epoch pilot data showing CWD monotonically increasing, directly contradicting Section 6.4 text which reports CWD as non-monotonic (H3 falsified from full 200-epoch data). The correct figure exists at exp/results/full/figures/fig04_batchsize_snr.pdf. Section 6.4 final paragraph still references 'monotonic CWD behavior' creating an internal contradiction.

6 of 8 planned figures are missing from paper body despite all being pre-generated at exp/results/full/figures/ (fig01-fig13). Figure 2 (UDWDC control loop) is a dead reference to a .md text file -- LaTeX compilation blocker. Figure 1 (rho trajectories, central to unification claim) is not referenced. The paper is assertion-heavy and evidence-light without its planned visualizations.
